TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit ECDsa extends System.Security.Cryptography.ECAlgorithm
Public MustInherit Class ECDsa Inherits ECAlgorithm

Docs.summary: Provides an abstract base class that encapsulates the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA).

remarks.format: ## Remarks This class serves as the abstract base class for <xref:System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsaCng> derivations.

MemberSignature.Value: protected ECDsa ();
.method familyhidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed
Protected Sub New ()
protected: ECDsa();

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This constructor is called by classes that derive from the <xref:System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa> class. The <xref:System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsaCng> class is the only implementation currently supported by .NET. You may provide your own custom implementation.

Docs.summary: Creates a new instance of an implementation of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA).

MemberSignature.Value: public static System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create ();
.method public static hidebysig class System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create() cil managed
Public Shared Function Create () As ECDsa
static member Create : unit -> System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa
public: static System::Security::Cryptography::ECDsa ^ Create();

Docs.summary: Creates a new instance of the default implementation of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA).
Docs.returns: A new instance of the default implementation () of this class.

MemberSignature.Value: public static System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create (System.Security.Cryptography.ECCurve curve);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create(valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.ECCurve curve) cil managed
Public Shared Function Create (curve As ECCurve) As ECDsa
static member Create : System.Security.Cryptography.ECCurve -> System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa
public: static System::Security::Cryptography::ECDsa ^ Create(System::Security::Cryptography::ECCurve curve);

Docs.param: The curve to use for key generation.
Docs.summary: Creates a new instance of the default implementation of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) with a newly generated key over the specified curve.
Docs.returns: A new instance of the default implementation () of this class.

MemberSignature.Value: public static System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create (System.Security.Cryptography.ECParameters parameters);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create(valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.ECParameters parameters) cil managed
Public Shared Function Create (parameters As ECParameters) As ECDsa
static member Create : System.Security.Cryptography.ECParameters -> System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa
public: static System::Security::Cryptography::ECDsa ^ Create(System::Security::Cryptography::ECParameters parameters);

Docs.param: The parameters representing the key to use.
Docs.summary: Creates a new instance of the default implementation of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) using the specified parameters as the key.
Docs.returns: A new instance of the default implementation () of this class.

MemberSignature.Value: public static System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa? Create (string algorithm);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa Create(string algorithm) cil managed
Public Shared Function Create (algorithm As String) As ECDsa
static member Create : string -> System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa
public: static System::Security::Cryptography::ECDsa ^ Create(System::String ^ algorithm);

Attribute.AttributeName: [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCode("The default algorithm implementations might be removed, use strong type references like 'RSA.Create()' instead.")]
[<System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCode("The default algorithm implementations might be removed, use strong type references like 'RSA.Create()' instead.")>]

[System.Obsolete("Cryptographic factory methods accepting an algorithm name are obsolete. Use the parameterless Create factory method on the algorithm type instead.", DiagnosticId="SYSLIB0045", UrlFormat="https://aka.ms/dotnet-warnings/{0}")]

[<System.Obsolete("Cryptographic factory methods accepting an algorithm name are obsolete. Use the parameterless Create factory method on the algorithm type instead.", DiagnosticId="SYSLIB0045", UrlFormat="https://aka.ms/dotnet-warnings/{0}")>]

Docs.param:
The name of an ECDSA implementation. The following strings all refer to the same implementation, which is the only implementation currently supported in .NET:
- "ECDsa" - "ECDsaCng" - "System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsaCng"
You can also provide the name of a custom ECDSA implementation.
Docs.summary: Creates a new instance of the specified implementation of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA).
Docs.returns: A new instance of the specified implementation of this class. If the specified algorithm name does not map to an ECDSA implementation, this method returns .

MemberSignature.Value: public override void FromXmlString (string xmlString);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance void FromXmlString(string xmlString) cil managed
Public Overrides Sub FromXmlString (xmlString As String)
override this.FromXmlString : string -> unit
public: override void FromXmlString(System::String ^ xmlString);

Docs.param: The XML string to use to reconstruct the object.
Docs.summary: This method throws in all cases.

MemberSignature.Value: public int GetMaxSignatureSize (System.Security.Cryptography.DSASignatureFormat signatureFormat);
.method public hidebysig instance int32 GetMaxSignatureSize(valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.DSASignatureFormat signatureFormat) cil managed
Public Function GetMaxSignatureSize (signatureFormat As DSASignatureFormat) As Integer
member this.GetMaxSignatureSize : System.Security.Cryptography.DSASignatureFormat -> int
public: int GetMaxSignatureSize(System::Security::Cryptography::DSASignatureFormat signatureFormat);

Docs.param: The encoding format for a signature.
Docs.summary: Gets the largest size, in bytes, for a signature produced by this key in the indicated format.
Docs.returns: The largest size, in bytes, for a signature produced by this key in the indicated format.

Docs.summary: Computes the hash value of binary data.

MemberSignature.Value: protected virtual byte[] HashData (System.IO.Stream data, System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm);
.method familyhidebysig newslot virtual instance unsigned int8[] HashData(class System.IO.Stream data, valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm) cil managed
Protected Overridable Function HashData (data As Stream, hashAlgorithm As HashAlgorithmName) As Byte()

abstract member HashData : System.IO.Stream * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName -> byte[] override this.HashData : System.IO.Stream * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName -> byte[]
protected: virtual cli::array <System::Byte> ^ HashData(System::IO::Stream ^ data, System::Security::Cryptography::HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm);

Docs.param: The binary stream to hash.
The algorithm to use to hash the data.
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, computes the hash value of the specified binary stream by using the specified hashing algorithm.
Docs.returns: The hashed data.
Docs.exception: A derived class must override this method.

MemberSignature.Value: protected virtual byte[] HashData (byte[] data, int offset, int count, System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm);

.method familyhidebysig newslot virtual instance unsigned int8[] HashData(unsigned int8[] data, int32 offset, int32 count, valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm) cil managed
Protected Overridable Function HashData (data As Byte(), offset As Integer, count As Integer, hashAlgorithm As HashAlgorithmName) As Byte()

abstract member HashData : byte[] * int * int * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName -> byte[] override this.HashData : byte[] * int * int * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName -> byte[]
protected: virtual cli::array <System::Byte> ^ HashData(cli::array <System::Byte> ^ data, int offset, int count, System::Security::Cryptography::HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm);

Docs.param: The data to be hashed.
The index of the first byte in to be hashed.
The number of bytes to hash.
The algorithm to use to hash the data.
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, computes the hash value of the specified portion of a byte array by using the specified hashing algorithm.
Docs.returns: The hashed data.
Docs.exception: A derived class must override this method.

Docs.summary: Imports the public/private keypair from a PKCS#8 EncryptedPrivateKeyInfo structure after decrypting with a byte-based password, replacing the keys for this object.
Imports an encrypted RFC 7468 PEM-encoded private key, replacing the keys for this object.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string? KeyExchangeAlgorithm { get; }
.property instance string KeyExchangeAlgorithm
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property KeyExchangeAlgorithm As String
member this.KeyExchangeAlgorithm : string
public: virtual property System::String ^ KeyExchangeAlgorithm { System::String ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the name of the key exchange algorithm.

remarks.format:
## Remarks This property is inherited from the <xref:System.Security.Cryptography.AsymmetricAlgorithm> base class.
The Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) is used for digital signature generation; it is not used for key derivation Therefore, this property returns `null`.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string SignatureAlgorithm { get; }
.property instance string SignatureAlgorithm
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property SignatureAlgorithm As String
member this.SignatureAlgorithm : string
public: virtual property System::String ^ SignatureAlgorithm { System::String ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the name of the signature algorithm.
Computes the hash value of the specified data and signs it.

Docs.param: The input data for which to compute the hash.
The hash algorithm to use to create the hash value.
Docs.summary: Computes the hash value of the specified byte array using the specified hash algorithm and signs the resulting hash value.
Docs.returns: The ECDSA signature for the specified data.

Docs.param: The data to hash and sign.
The buffer to receive the signature.
The hash algorithm to use to create the hash value.
Docs.summary: Computes the hash value of the specified data and signs it in the indicated format into the provided buffer.
Docs.returns: The total number of bytes written to .
Docs.remarks: This method will use to encode the the signature. To use a different signature format, use .
Docs.exception:
's is an empty string.
-or-
The buffer in is too small to hold the signature.

The buffer to receive the signature.
The encoding format to use for the signature.
Docs.summary: Computes the ECDSA signature for the specified hash value in the indicated format into the provided buffer.
Docs.returns: The total number of bytes written to .
Docs.exception: An error occurred in the signing operation.
The buffer in is too small to hold the signature.

MemberSignature.Value: protected virtual byte[] SignHashCore (ReadOnlySpan<byte> hash, System.Security.Cryptography.DSASignatureFormat signatureFormat);

Docs.param: The hash value to sign.
The encoding format to use for the signature.
Docs.summary: Computes the ECDSA signature for the specified hash value in the indicated format.
Docs.returns: The ECDSA signature for the specified data.
Docs.exception: An error occurred in the signing operation.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string ToXmlString (bool includePrivateParameters);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance string ToXmlString(bool includePrivateParameters) cil managed
Public Overrides Function ToXmlString (includePrivateParameters As Boolean) As String
override this.ToXmlString : bool -> string
public: override System::String ^ ToXmlString(bool includePrivateParameters);

Docs.param: to include private parameters; otherwise, .
Docs.summary: This method throws in all cases.
Docs.returns: This method does not return a value.

Docs.summary: Attempts to export the current key in the PKCS#8 EncryptedPrivateKeyInfo format into a provided buffer, using a byte-based password.

MemberSignature.Value: protected virtual bool TryHashData (ReadOnlySpan<byte> data, Span<byte> destination, System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm, out int bytesWritten);

.method familyhidebysig newslot virtual instance bool TryHashData(valuetype System.ReadOnlySpan`1<unsigned int8> data, valuetype System.Span`1<unsigned int8> destination, valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm, [out] int32& bytesWritten) cil managed
Protected Overridable Function TryHashData (data As ReadOnlySpan(Of Byte), destination As Span(Of Byte), hashAlgorithm As HashAlgorithmName, ByRef bytesWritten As Integer) As Boolean

abstract member TryHashData : ReadOnlySpan<byte> * Span<byte> * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName * int -> bool override this.TryHashData : ReadOnlySpan<byte> * Span<byte> * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName * int -> bool

protected: virtual bool TryHashData(ReadOnlySpan<System::Byte> data, Span<System::Byte> destination, System::Security::Cryptography::HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm, [Runtime::InteropServices::Out] int % bytesWritten);

Docs.param: The data to be hashed.
The buffer to receive the hash value.
The algorithm to use to hash the data.
When this method returns, contains the total number of bytes written into . This parameter is treated as uninitialized.
Docs.summary: Attempts to compute the hash value of the specified read-only span of bytes into the provided destination by using the specified hashing algorithm.
Docs.returns: if is not long enough to receive the hash value.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
The default implementation of this method is to call <xref:System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa.HashData(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName)> and copy the result to `destination`. Derived types should override this method to avoid the intermediate array creation.

Docs.summary: Attempts to create the ECDSA signature for the specified data in the indicated format into the provided buffer.

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool TrySignData (ReadOnlySpan<byte> data, Span<byte> destination, System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm, out int bytesWritten);

.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance bool TrySignData(valuetype System.ReadOnlySpan`1<unsigned int8> data, valuetype System.Span`1<unsigned int8> destination, valuetype System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm, [out] int32& bytesWritten) cil managed
Public Overridable Function TrySignData (data As ReadOnlySpan(Of Byte), destination As Span(Of Byte), hashAlgorithm As HashAlgorithmName, ByRef bytesWritten As Integer) As Boolean

abstract member TrySignData : ReadOnlySpan<byte> * Span<byte> * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName * int -> bool override this.TrySignData : ReadOnlySpan<byte> * Span<byte> * System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName * int -> bool

public: virtual bool TrySignData(ReadOnlySpan<System::Byte> data, Span<System::Byte> destination, System::Security::Cryptography::HashAlgorithmName hashAlgorithm, [Runtime::InteropServices::Out] int % bytesWritten);

Docs.param: The data to be signed.
The buffer to receive the signature.
The algorithm to use to hash the data for signing.
When this method returns, contains the total number of bytes written into . This parameter is treated as uninitialized.
Docs.summary: Attempts to compute the ECDSA digital signature for the specified read-only span of bytes into the provided destination by using the specified hashing algorithm and the current key.
Docs.returns: if is not long enough to receive the signature.

Docs.param: The data to hash and sign.
The buffer to receive the signature.
The hash algorithm to use to create the hash value.
The encoding format to use for the signature.
When this method returns, contains a value that indicates the number of bytes written to . This parameter is treated as uninitialized.
Docs.summary: Attempts to create the ECDSA signature for the specified data in the indicated format into the provided buffer.
Docs.returns: if is big enough to receive the signature; otherwise, .
Docs.exception: An error occurred in the signing operation.

The buffer to receive the signature.
When this method returns, contains the total number of bytes written into . This parameter is treated as uninitialized.
Docs.summary: Attempts to compute the ECDSA digital signature for the specified read-only span of bytes representing a data hash into the provided destination by using the current key.
Docs.returns: if is not long enough to receive the signature.

is less than zero.

+ - 1 results in an index that is beyond the upper bound of .
